abstract.text: This article describes the KeyboardKit layout engine.

inlineContent.text: A flexible keyboard layout is an important part of a software keyboard, and must consider many factors like the current locale, device, screen orientation, user preferences, etc.
In KeyboardKit, an
defines the input keys of a keyboard, after which a
can create a dynamic
at runtime that defines the full set of keys.
unlocks localized layout services for all
locales in your license, additional input sets like
, iPad Pro support, more layout capabilities that make modifying layouts easier, etc. Read more further down.
KeyboardKit has a
type that is also a namespace for other layout-related types like
, etc.
In KeyboardKit an
specifies the input keys of a keyboard, while a
specifies the full set of keys. Layouts can vary greatly for different devices, screens, locales, etc.
KeyboardKit comes with pre-defined input sets, like
. KeyboardKit Pro unlocks more input sets, like
and
, as well as locale-specific input sets for all
locales.

While most iOS keyboards have 3 input rows of input keys surrounded by action keys, and a bottom row with space bar and action keys, this is not true for all locales. The layout can vary greatly, so the layout engine must be flexible.
In KeyboardKit, a
can generate dynamic layouts at runtime, It provides us with the flexibility we need, to accomodate to the varying needs for different locales, devices, etc.
KeyboardKit injects
into
. You can replace it at any time, as described further down, or add custom services to it with
unlocks more input sets, like
, as well as alphabetic, numeric & symbolic sets for all
locales that are included in your license, like
KeyboardKit Pro also unlocks a localized
for every locale in your license and injects them as localized services into the main
when a valid license is registered. You can access any localized service in your license like this:
KeyboardKit Pro unlocks an
that can generate iPad Pro-specific layouts for most supported locales.
KeyboardKit Pro extends the
property and all layout
collections with more capabilities, that make it easier to insert, remove, and replace items and actions.
KeyboardKit Pro also unlocks more
capabilities like
and
, and a specific set of tools for the bottom row, like
See
and its

in the KeyboardKit Pro documentations for a full list of capabilities that KeyboardKit Pro unlocks. The open-source documentation doesn’t include all these extensions, which is why they are not in the documentation.
You can create a custom
to customize the layout for a certain locale, or define a fully custom layout.
You can implement the
protocol from scratch, or inherit and customize
